# Swarm Documentation

> Welcome to the Swarm

This file contains links to documentation sections following the llmstxt.org standard.

## Table of Contents

- [Bee FAQ](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/faq): Addresses common questions about running Bee nodes including setup installation troubleshooting and blockchain interactions.
- [Build from Source](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/installation/build-from-source): Guides developers through compiling Bee directly from source code using Go git and make with step-by-step instructions.
- [Connectivity](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/installation/connectivity): Explains network setup and NAT configuration to ensure Bee nodes can communicate with peers on both private and public networks.
- [Docker Install](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/installation/docker): Provides comprehensive steps for deploying Bee nodes using Docker containers with volume management and network configuration.
- [Fund Your Node](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/installation/fund-your-node): Outlines xDAI and xBZZ token requirements by use case and provides guidance on acquiring tokens from exchanges and faucets.
- [Getting Started](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/installation/getting-started): Introduces Bee node types their requirements and available installation methods to help users choose appropriate setup approaches.
- [Hive](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/installation/hive): Describes tools and orchestration methods for managing multiple Bee nodes using Docker Compose Helm or manual configuration.
- [Package Manager Install](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/installation/package-manager): Guides installation using system package managers (APT RPM Homebrew) with background service configuration and management.
- [Quickstart](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/installation/quick-start): Accelerates Bee setup with shell script installation and swarm-cli tools enabling rapid node deployment and network interaction.
- [Set Target Neighborhood](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/installation/set-target-neighborhood): Explains how to strategically assign node neighborhoods using Swarmscan data to optimize staking rewards and network resilience.
- [Shell Script Install](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/installation/shell-script): Provides flexible installation using an automated shell script supporting Linux and macOS with customizable configuration options.
- [Verify](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/installation/verify): Reference documentation for node verification procedures.
- [Backups](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/working-with-bee/backups): Details critical backup procedures for keys password and node data across various installation methods and platforms.
- [Bcrypt hashing utility](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/working-with-bee/bcrypt): Shows how to generate and validate bcrypt password hashes using Bee's built-in utilities or external tools.
- [Bee API](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/working-with-bee/bee-api): Comprehensive reference for Bee's HTTP API endpoints enabling programmatic access to node management uploads downloads and monitoring.
- [Cashing Out](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/working-with-bee/cashing-out): Explains how to withdraw earned xBZZ rewards and manage cheques through the bandwidth incentives SWAP system.
- [Configuration](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/working-with-bee/configuration): Documents all Bee configuration options available through YAML files environment variables and command-line flags.
- [Introduction](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/working-with-bee/introduction): Overview of node operation topics including configuration API access backups monitoring and upgrade procedures.
- [Logging in Bee](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/working-with-bee/logs-and-files): Guides log access rotation verbosity levels and structured logging integration with monitoring tools like Prometheus and Grafana.
- [Monitoring Your Node](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/working-with-bee/monitoring): Explains how to monitor Bee node metrics using Prometheus and Grafana for tracking cheque rates and network performance.
- [Node Types](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/working-with-bee/node-types): Compares full light and ultra-light node types with their features requirements and configuration for different use cases.
- [Staking](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/working-with-bee/staking): Walkthrough of depositing xBZZ to participate in the storage incentives redistribution game and earn network rewards.
- [Swarm CLI](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/working-with-bee/swarm-cli): Introduces swarm-cli command-line tool that simplifies node interaction uploads downloads and batch management.
- [Uninstalling Bee](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/working-with-bee/uninstalling-bee): Instructions for cleanly removing Bee installations across different operating systems and package managers.
- [Upgrading Bee](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/bee/working-with-bee/upgrade): Procedures for safely upgrading Bee to latest versions while preserving keys avoiding round disruption and cashing out rewards.
- [DISC](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/concepts/DISC/DISC): Overview of Swarm's Distributed Immutable Storage of Chunks system using Kademlia neighborhoods and synchronization protocols.
- [Erasure Coding](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/concepts/DISC/erasure-coding): Explains optional data protection technique using redundant chunks at multiple protection levels to ensure reliable data recovery.
- [Kademlia](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/concepts/DISC/kademlia): Details distributed hash table algorithm using XOR distance metrics for efficient decentralized lookups and network routing.
- [Neighborhoods](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/concepts/DISC/neighborhoods): Describes proximity-based node groupings that share storage responsibilities using proximity order to determine neighborhoods.
- [Access Control](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/concepts/access-control): Introduces Access Control Trie (ACT) for managing encryption and permissions in decentralized storage with content sharing capabilities.
- [Bandwidth Incentives (SWAP)](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/concepts/incentives/bandwidth-incentives): Explains SWAP protocol for managing bandwidth resource exchange between nodes using cheques and off-chain accounting.
- [Incentives Overview](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/concepts/incentives/overview): Describes dual incentive mechanisms combining storage incentives for data retention and bandwidth incentives for data relay.
- [Postage Stamps](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/concepts/incentives/postage-stamps): Details prepaid batch system for uploading data to Swarm with dynamic pricing based on network redundancy signals.
- [Price Oracle](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/concepts/incentives/price-oracle): Describes smart contract mechanism for dynamically adjusting postage stamp prices based on network utilization data.
- [Redistribution Game](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/concepts/incentives/redistribution-game): Explains game-theoretic system distributing xBZZ from postage stamps to full nodes that honestly store data.
- [Introduction](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/concepts/introduction): Overview of Swarm's peer-to-peer infrastructure the Bee client and the Swarm Foundation's mission for decentralized storage.
- [PSS](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/concepts/pss): Explains Postal Service over Swarm messaging protocol enabling secure private and efficient communication between network nodes.
- [What is Swarm?](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/concepts/what-is-swarm): Details Swarm's four-layer architecture including underlay overlay data access and application layers for decentralized storage.
- [Access Content](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/desktop/access-content): Guide for using Swarm Desktop application to download and retrieve content from the Swarm network.
- [Backup and Restore](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/desktop/backup-restore): Instructions for backing up and restoring wallet keys and data in the Swarm Desktop application.
- [Configuration](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/desktop/configuration): Explains configuration options and settings available in the Swarm Desktop user interface.
- [Install](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/desktop/install): Provides installation instructions for the Swarm Desktop GUI application on Windows macOS and Linux.
- [Introduction](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/desktop/introduction): Overview of Swarm Desktop graphical interface for managing nodes uploading and downloading content.
- [Postage Stamps](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/desktop/postage-stamps): Guide for purchasing and managing postage stamp batches through the Swarm Desktop interface.
- [Publish a Website](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/desktop/publish-a-website): Step-by-step instructions for uploading and hosting websites on Swarm using the Desktop application.
- [Start a Blog](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/desktop/start-a-blog): Tutorial for creating and publishing blog content on Swarm with the Desktop application.
- [Upload Content](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/desktop/upload-content): Instructions for uploading files and directories to Swarm through the Desktop application interface.
- [Add Access Control](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/access-control): Guide for implementing encryption and access control in decentralized applications using Bee.
- [Introduction](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/contribute/introduction): Overview of how to contribute to Bee development including code standards and contribution process.
- [Protocols](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/contribute/protocols): Technical documentation of core Bee protocols and their implementation details for developers.
- [Dynamic Content](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/dynamic-content): Learn how to use feeds to create updateable content on Swarm — with a complete example project that builds a simple blog.
- [Manage Files](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/files): Guide for working with file operations including chunking and handling in Swarm applications.
- [Run a Gateway](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/gateway): Explains Bee gateway functionality for accessing Swarm content through HTTP interfaces.
- [Host a Webpage](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/host-your-website): Comprehensive guide for uploading and hosting websites on Swarm with content addressing.
- [Start Building](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/introduction): Overview of development on Swarm including APIs tools and use cases for building applications.
- [Multi-Author Blog](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/multi-author-blog): Build a decentralized multi-author blog on Swarm using linked feeds — each author has their own feed, and a master index feed ties them together.
- [Website Routing](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/routing): Explains message routing protocols and peer discovery mechanisms in the Swarm network.
- [Bee JS](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/tools-and-features/bee-js): Documentation for the JavaScript library providing programmatic access to Bee node APIs.
- [Postage Stamp Batches](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/tools-and-features/buy-a-stamp-batch): Guide for purchasing postage stamp batches required for uploading data to Swarm.
- [Chunk Types](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/tools-and-features/chunk-types): Explains different chunk types including content-addressed and single-owner chunks used in Swarm.
- [Developer mode](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/tools-and-features/dev-mode): Documentation for development mode features for testing and debugging Bee applications.
- [Erasure Coding](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/tools-and-features/erasure-coding): Guide for using optional erasure coding to add redundancy and protection to uploaded data.
- [Feeds](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/tools-and-features/feeds): Explains mutable content feeds allowing for updating content while maintaining a static address.
- [Gateway Proxy](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/tools-and-features/gateway-proxy): Tool for proxying and protecting Bee API endpoints with additional security and filtering.
- [GSOC](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/tools-and-features/gsoc): Documentation for Group Send Over Content messaging feature in Swarm.
- [Introduction](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/tools-and-features/introduction): Overview of advanced tools and features available for Swarm development.
- [Manifests](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/tools-and-features/manifests): Guide for using manifests to organize and address multiple files as a single unit in Swarm.
- [Pinning](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/tools-and-features/pinning): Explains pinning mechanism for ensuring data permanence and preventing garbage collection.
- [PSS Messaging](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/tools-and-features/pss): Guide for using Postal Service over Swarm for private messaging between nodes.
- [Starting a Private Network](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/tools-and-features/starting-a-test-network): Instructions for setting up local test networks for development and experimentation.
- [Store with Encryption](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/tools-and-features/store-with-encryption): Guide for encrypting data before upload to protect privacy and confidentiality.
- [Ultra Light Nodes](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/ultra-light-nodes): Guide for running minimal ultra-light nodes with limited functionality and resource requirements.
- [Upload & Download](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/develop/upload-and-download): Comprehensive guide for uploading and downloading files with the Bee API.
- [Awesome Swarm](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/references/awesome-list): Curated list of community resources tools and projects related to Swarm.
- [Community](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/references/community): Information about Swarm community channels resources and contribution opportunities.
- [Fair Data Society](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/references/fair-data-society): Overview of Fair Data Society initiatives and collaboration with Swarm.
- [FAQ](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/references/faq): Frequently asked questions about Swarm protocols and general usage.
- [Glossary](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/references/glossary): Comprehensive glossary of terms and concepts used throughout Swarm documentation.
- [Smart Contracts](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/references/smart-contracts): Reference documentation for Swarm smart contracts including addresses and ABIs.
- [Tokens](https://docs.ethswarm.org/docs/references/tokens): Information about xBZZ and xDAI tokens including where to obtain them.
